steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
Sum from the frequency table:
$$13(12)+14(8)+15(5)+16(7)+17(9)+18(10)+19(13)+20(7)=1175$$
Original mean: $\frac{1175}{71}$. New mean after adding $39$: $\frac{1175+39}{72}=\frac{1214}{72}$. Desmos can compute both means directly; the new mean is greater.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content:
Original (71 values): Median is at position $\frac{71+1}{2} = 36$. Cumulative frequencies 12, 20, 25, 32, 41 put the 36th value in the weight-17 group, so the original median is 17.

New (72 values): The added value 39 is the largest, so it occupies position 72. Positions 36 and 37 still hold weight 17, giving a new median of $\frac{17+17}{2} = 17$.

The medians are equal.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A — wrong. Claims the median increased; the medians are equal.
  • B — correct. New mean is greater; medians are equal.
  • C — wrong. Claims the mean decreased; the new mean is greater.
  • D — wrong. Claims the mean decreased; the new mean is greater.
That matches choice B.
